texto: Each of us would like the ability to do what we want to do, when we want to do it, without incurring the moral approbation of others. We, however, tend to conveniently forget this also gives others the right to do whatever they want.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stephen McAndrew: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Each of us would like the ability to do what we want to do, when we want to do it, without incurr…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, ethics, morality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e morality.

texto: Christianity tells us we have free will. God has provided man with a choice whether to believe in Him or not. If God's existence were logically inescapable, there would be no free will to choose whether or not to believe in Him.

texto: If truth and moral values are relative, one cannot claim that certain human rights are universally applicable to all cultures and all people.

texto: We are fed ideas in small sound bites that are really just the conclusions of particular beliefs. We do not examine what underpins these sound bites. If the sound bites are presented by a source we are accustomed to accepting as true, there is a danger we will assimilate the conclusion without knowing, or caring, whether it is based on solid arguments and assumptions.

texto: Given our obsession with self, it is hardly surprising we think it is fine for us to live in a world with malleable moral markers, as long as we get our own way without being bullied by others into accepting their way of doing things. We want others to respect moral boundaries that we want to be free to ignore when it suits.

texto: The reason many are loathe to acknowledge the possibility of absolute truth is not simply because they do not wish to accept the possibility of the existence of an all-powerful, all-knowing deity. It is because they do not want to accept the consequences that follow from the existence of an all-powerful, all-knowing deity as the source of absolute moral truth.
